summary: CI workflow has a step ordering bug: corepack enable must run before actions/setup-node when cache: pnpm is set, otherwise the cache key computation fails because pnpm is not yet available. Additionally, node version 22 is hardcoded with no .node-version file to keep CI and local dev in sync.

suggestions.description: corepack enable runs after actions/setup-node with cache: pnpm, but setup-node needs the pnpm binary available to compute the cache key from the lockfile
suggestions.rationale: When actions/setup-node runs with cache: pnpm, it invokes pnpm to resolve the lockfile path and hash it. If corepack has not yet enabled pnpm, this either fails or silently skips caching, defeating the purpose of the cache configuration.
suggestions.suggestion: Move the corepack enable step before actions/setup-node, or split into two setup-node steps: one without cache (to install node), then corepack enable, then a separate actions/cache step for pnpm.

suggestions.description: Node version 22 is hardcoded in the workflow with no corresponding .node-version or .nvmrc file in the repo
suggestions.rationale: Without a single source of truth for the Node version, CI and local development environments can drift apart silently.
suggestions.suggestion: Create a .node-version file with the value 22 and reference it in the workflow using node-version-file instead of a hardcoded value.
